title: Notes from a battery RUL project
excerpt: An R² of 0.993 looked great until I asked where it came from. A short write-up on leakage, feature honesty, and whether a model deserves trust.

pageSections.title: Trust the audit, not the headline
pageSections.markdown: A near-perfect score on a noisy target is a smell before it is a celebration. The real work in this kind of project is often the audit: whether the feature story makes sense, whether leakage is distorting the result, and whether the explanation of the model aligns with the system being modeled.
